deal.next_step: 6.29.26 KK - had commercial call. Pricing well received. They understand the timeline is tight and they need to make a decision in the coming week or so. RISK- coupa is going onsite wednesday. sharing commercials with value slides on why zycus for platform and partnership offering executive session a Aatish / Bikash 6/22mc- Followed up with Bass Pro to re-engage David and Whitney on the updated proposal. Left David a voicemail and also sent Whitney a text requesting 20–30 minutes with both of them to review the proposal, confirm alignment on scope/pricing, and define next steps. Current risk is increasing as the account has been unresponsive for over a week, despite the proposal being ready for review. Strategy is to use the pending customer reference conversations as a reason to re-engage David and create urgency around the evaluation. If no response is received by Wednesday, June 24, we will escalate thoughtfully by expanding outreach to additional executive stakeholders, including the CFO and CPO/David’s boss, to request an executive alignment meeting. To date, we have held back on broader executive outreach because David indicated the process should move under his direction, but lack of engagement may require us to create additional visibility and momentum to protect the deal .

competition.summary: A declared final two: Zycus versus Coupa, the six-year incumbent (David Elford, 27 May: 'it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent'). Coupa's position is defensive but strong: 2,000 entrenched daily P2P users, a contract already extended once to Feb 2027, zero implementation cost, and a retention onsite scheduled for 1 Jul (logged 29 Jun), landing inside the buyer's 6 to 13 Jul decision windo

champion: David Elford is a strong, directive champion: he has anchored every buyer session from 12 Mar to 29 Jun, pulled new stakeholders in mid-meeting (Chris, 27 May), argued internally for the larger scope (6 Apr), proposed the 29 Jun commercial review himself, and set the one-week decision clock. fit.positioning_strength: AI is central to the pitch and landed as a differentiator, not a gimmick: Merlin Intake inside Microsoft Teams anchored the 30 Mar demo ('your whole intake capability is intriguing as well', David Elford), AP Smartdesk AI extraction read as
